---
title: "Washington Highway CMF Analysis"
subtitle: "Hierarchical Crash Modification Factors"
author: "MetaCount Research Group"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.05
    self-contained: true
  pptx:
    slide-level: 2
execute:
  enabled: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
:root { --teal:#0a6c74; --orange:#d96f32; --ink:#152238; --muted:#5d6b79; --paper:#f8f6f2; }
.reveal h1, .reveal h2 { color: var(--teal); font-size: 1.5rem; margin-bottom: 4px; }
.reveal h3 { color: var(--orange); font-size: 1.05rem; margin: 0 0 6px; }
.reveal h2::after { content:""; display:block; height:3px; width:50px;
  background:var(--orange); margin-top:3px; border-radius:2px; }
.reveal .title-slide h1 { font-size:2rem; color:#fff; }
.reveal .title-slide { background:linear-gradient(135deg,#0a6c74 0%,#18435a 55%,#8a4e2c 100%); }
/* Body text */
.reveal section { font-size: 0.82rem; line-height: 1.35; }
/* Stat boxes — compact */
.sb { background:var(--paper); border-left:4px solid var(--teal); border-radius:5px;
      padding:5px 12px; margin:3px 0; }
.sb .n { font-size:1.5rem; font-weight:800; color:var(--teal); }
.sb .l { color:var(--muted); font-size:0.7rem; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:.06em; }
/* Tables */
table { font-size:0.72rem; width:100%; border-collapse:collapse; }
th { background:var(--teal); color:#fff; padding:4px 8px; text-align:left; }
td { padding:3px 8px; vertical-align:top; word-wrap:break-word; }
tr:nth-child(even) td { background:#eef6f7; }
/* Callout */
.co { border-left:4px solid var(--orange); background:#fff8f3;
      padding:5px 12px; border-radius:0 5px 5px 0; font-size:0.78rem; margin:5px 0; }
/* Better / worse badges */
.better { color:#2a7f4f; font-weight:700; }
.worse  { color:#b93a2b; font-weight:700; }
/* iframe */
iframe { border:none; border-radius:6px; box-shadow:0 3px 10px rgba(0,0,0,.12); }
/* Columns fix */
.reveal .columns { display:flex; gap:16px; }
.reveal ul li { margin-bottom:0.2em; }
</style>
```

## Data Overview

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="38%"}
<div class="sb"><div class="n">275</div><div class="l">road segments</div></div>
<div class="sb"><div class="n">39</div><div class="l">variables per segment</div></div>
<div class="sb"><div class="n">FREQ</div><div class="l">crash count — NB2 response</div></div>
<div class="sb"><div class="n">AADT</div><div class="l">primary traffic exposure</div></div>
<div class="sb"><div class="n">LENGTH</div><div class="l">segment length → log(L) offset</div></div>
:::

::: {.column width="62%"}
| Category | Variables |
|---|---|
| **Traffic** | AADT, PEAKHR, ADTLANE, EXPOSE |
| **Geometry** | INCLANES, DECLANES, WIDTH, MEDWIDTH |
| **Alignment** | CURVES, MINRAD, TANGENT, GRADEBR |
| **Grade** | MIGRADE, MXGRADE, MXGRDIFF, SLOPE |
| **Roadside** | MIMEDSH, MXMEDSH, ACCESS, FRICTION |
| **Climate** | AVEPRE, AVESNOW, HISNOW, LOWPRE |
| **Class** | URB, FC, SINGLE, DOUBLE, TRAIN |
| **Auxiliary** | INTPM, CPM, INTECHAG, EXPOSE |

Source: Washington State DOT rural highway inventory
:::

::::

---

## Methodology

$$
\log(\hat{\mu}_i) = \alpha
  + \underbrace{\beta_{\text{AADT}}\log(\text{AADT}_i)}_{\text{AADT elasticity}}
  + \underbrace{\sum_{j} \beta_j x_{ij}}_{\text{Upper: direct}}
  + \underbrace{\sum_{k} \gamma_k x_{ik}\log(\text{AADT}_i)}_{\text{Lower: modify elasticity}}
  + \log(L_i)
$$

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Upper terms** — AADT-independent effect.
CMF for a 1-unit change: $\text{CMF} = e^{\beta_j}$. Same across all AADT levels.

**Lower terms** — modify the AADT elasticity:
$$e_i = \beta_{\text{AADT}} + \sum_k \gamma_k x_{ik} > 0 \;\text{(enforced)}$$
CMF varies with traffic volume.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Random search strategy**

1. **Random sampling** (120 iterations): draw $k_U \in [0,8]$ upper and $k_L \in [0,6]$ lower variables at random from the full candidate pool
2. Score each candidate on validation set by **Poisson deviance** (primary) and **RMSE** (secondary); reject any spec with non-monotonic AADT elasticity
3. **Final refit** on train + validation (80%); evaluate on held-out test set (20%)
4. **Post-search random-parameters refit** on best spec using JAX mixed-NB2
:::

::::

---

## Search Convergence

::: {.content-visible when-format="revealjs"}
<iframe src="../results/ex16_3_cmf/search_convergence.html"
  width="100%" height="500px" title="Convergence"></iframe>
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pptx"}
![Convergence: proposed-path trace (dotted) + running best solid envelope for BIC (left) and Validation RMSE (right). Green = monotonic AADT, grey = non-monotonic (excluded), star = global best.](../results/ex16_3_cmf/search_convergence.png)
:::

Dotted line = sequence of all proposed models · Solid envelope = running best · Green = monotonic AADT · Grey = excluded (non-monotonic) · Star = global best

---

## Model Coefficients — Core & Upper Terms {.scrollable}

### Direct effects on crash rate (AADT-independent), with CMF at 1-SD change

{{< include ../results/ex16_3_cmf/readable_coef_upper.md >}}

<div class="co">CMF < 1 = safety benefit &nbsp;|&nbsp; CMF > 1 = higher risk &nbsp;|&nbsp; All continuous variables standardised; CMF shown at 1-SD change.</div>

---

## Model Coefficients — Lower Terms {.scrollable}

### Variables that modify how sensitive crash risk is to traffic volume

{{< include ../results/ex16_3_cmf/readable_coef_lower.md >}}

<div class="co"><b>Interpretation:</b> positive coefficient → amplifies AADT elasticity → variable makes crash risk more traffic-sensitive. Negative → buffers against volume-driven risk.</div>

---

## Benchmark Comparison

### Hierarchical CMF vs Simple AADT-only Safety Performance Function

{{< include ../results/ex16_3_cmf/benchmark_comparison.md >}}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Benchmark model** (standard practice)

$$\log(\hat{\mu}) = \alpha + \beta_{\text{AADT}}\log(\text{AADT}) + \log(L)$$

No road geometry, weather, or traffic interaction terms — the minimum viable SPF used in many agency applications.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**What lower BIC / RMSE / Deviance means**

- **BIC** penalises complexity — a lower value with more parameters indicates genuine information gain, not overfitting
- **Test RMSE** is unbiased (held-out data not used in fitting or selection)
- **Test R²** fraction of crash variance explained on unseen segments
:::

::::

---

## Model Comparison — Baseline vs Hierarchical

### Observed crashes + both predicted curves on the same AADT axes

::: {.content-visible when-format="revealjs"}
<iframe src="../results/ex16_3_cmf/model_comparison.html"
  width="100%" height="490px" title="Model comparison"></iframe>
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pptx"}
![Observed crashes (dots by split) vs polynomial-smooth predictions from the AADT-only benchmark (blue dashed) and the hierarchical CMF model (orange solid). Bottom panel shows normalised residuals for each model vs AADT.](../results/ex16_3_cmf/model_comparison.png)
:::

---

## AADT Response — Hierarchical Model

### Observed vs hierarchical predictions with normalised residuals

::: {.content-visible when-format="revealjs"}
<iframe src="../results/ex16_3_cmf/aadt_obs_pred.html"
  width="100%" height="490px" title="AADT obs vs pred"></iframe>
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pptx"}
![Observed crashes vs hierarchical model polynomial smooth. Bottom: normalised residuals — orange = over-predicted, teal = under-predicted.](../results/ex16_3_cmf/aadt_obs_pred.png)
:::

---

## CMF Interpretation

$$\text{CMF} = \frac{\hat{\mu}(\text{modified})}{\hat{\mu}(\text{baseline})} \qquad \text{CMF}<1 = \text{safer} \qquad \text{CMF}>1 = \text{higher risk}$$

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Upper-level CMF** — constant across all AADT levels

Effect is the same whether the road carries 3,000 or 30,000 vehicles per day. A high-snow-days coefficient of +0.02 means every additional snow day multiplies crash risk by e^0.02 = 1.02 regardless of volume.

**Lower-level CMF** — traffic-amplified

Each unit of the variable shifts the AADT elasticity: $e_i = \beta_{\text{AADT}} + \gamma_k x_{ik}$. More curves → higher elasticity → crash rate grows faster with AADT. This is the *key innovation* over standard SPFs.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Functional Classification (FC) reference**

| FC Code | Road Type |
|---|---|
| 1 | Principal Arterial — Interstate |
| 2 | Principal Arterial — Other |
| 3 | Minor Arterial |
| 4 | Major Collector |
| 5 | Minor Collector |
| 6 | Local Road |

<div class="co">The hierarchical model captures <b>effect modification</b>: the same road geometry can have very different safety implications depending on how much traffic it carries.</div>
:::

::::

---

## Interactive CMF Dashboard

::: {.content-visible when-format="revealjs"}
<iframe src="../results/ex16_3_cmf/hierarchical_cmf_dashboard.html"
  width="100%" height="520px" title="CMF Dashboard"></iframe>
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pptx"}
**Interactive CMF Dashboard** — open `results/ex16_3_cmf/hierarchical_cmf_dashboard.html` in any browser.

Features:

- **Variable dropdown** — select any model variable to plot its CMF response curve
- **Mode toggle** — switch between *Predicted crashes* and *CMF* (ratio to baseline)
- **AADT slider** — shift between low-volume and high-volume traffic levels
- **Live CMF badge** on each control — updates in real time as you adjust inputs
- **Prediction Summary** panel — shows current predicted crash count + AADT elasticity
:::

Select any variable · Toggle CMF vs Predicted · Adjust AADT · Live Prediction Summary panel updates in real time

---

## Key Findings & Benefits

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="55%"}
**Methodological contributions**

1. **Two-level structure** separates baseline crash rate from traffic-sensitivity — enables AADT-varying CMFs that standard SPFs cannot produce
2. **Bi-objective random search** tracks both RMSE and Poisson deviance simultaneously — selects the specification that minimises validation deviance while holding AADT monotonicity
3. **AADT monotonicity constraint** — every segment must show more crashes with more traffic (enforced during search, not post-hoc)
4. **JAX NB2 engine** — exact autodiff gradients, consistent with MetaCount mixed-model infrastructure, enables seamless extension to random parameters

**Benchmark improvement**

Lower BIC, lower test RMSE, higher R² than the AADT-only SPF — achieved with interpretable geometry and climate effects, not black-box complexity.
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
**Output files**

| File | Content |
|---|---|
| `hierarchical_cmf_dashboard.html` | Live CMF explorer |
| `search_convergence.html/.png` | Convergence (both objectives) |
| `aadt_obs_pred.html/.png` | Obs vs pred — smooth curve |
| `benchmark_comparison.csv` | Hierarchical vs SPF metrics |
| `readable_coefficients.csv` | Full name + CMF table |
| `model_summary_readable.txt` | Plain-text model summary |
| `final_model_spec.json` | Coefficients + metadata |
| `validation_metrics.csv` | Train/val/test breakdown |

**Run**
```bash
python build_washington_presentation.py
# → HTML + PPTX in ~3 minutes
```
:::

::::
